articles.title: Samsung quadruples monthly DRAM supply to Tesla- report
articles.summary: Samsung Electronics has quadrupled its monthly supply of 8GB GDDR6 DRAM to Tesla Inc. in April, responding to increased memory demand from the electric vehicle maker for its in-vehicle infotainment and autonomous driving systems. This surge comes amid tightening global memory chip supplies and Samsung's plans to produce advanced AI chips for Tesla at its Texas foundry in late 2026.
articles.text_to_analyze: Samsung quadruples monthly DRAM supply to Tesla- report. articles.title: NIO Gives Its Model Y Nemesis LiDAR Vision — Wallet-Friendly Price Tag Unchanged
articles.summary: Nio Inc. has launched an updated version of its Onvo L90 SUV, its mass-market offering, which now includes a LiDAR sensor for enhanced assisted driving capabilities without an increase in its wallet-friendly price of RMB 265,800. This move by Nio contrasts with Tesla CEO Elon Musk's stance against LiDAR in favor of camera-only vision for assisted driving. The Onvo L90 continues to be priced significantly lower than Tesla's Model Y L in China, and Nio is expanding its Onvo sub-brand with a new five-seat SUV, the L80, slated for release in Q2.

articles.title: YieldMax Short TSLA Option Income Strategy ETF declares $0.0879 dividend
articles.summary: The YieldMax Short TSLA Option Income Strategy ETF (TSLY) has declared a cash dividend of $0.0879 per share. Shareholders on record as of April 24, 2026, will receive this dividend, which is payable on April 26, 2026. The ex-dividend date for this payout is April 24, 2026.

articles.title: Morgan Stanley resets Intel stock price target ahead of earnings
articles.summary: Morgan Stanley has raised its price target for Intel (INTC) stock to $56 from $41, maintaining an equal-weight (hold) rating, ahead of its Q1 2026 earnings report. This adjustment comes after Intel's stock surged 78% year-to-date, driven by key strategic deals with Apollo, Tesla/SpaceX, and Google Cloud, which have fueled optimism despite a previous dip after disappointing Q4 earnings guidance. The analysts acknowledge significant upside potential from foundry partnerships and regaining market share, while also flagging risks from AMD competition and potential inefficiencies in its foundry business.

articles.title: GM Gave $40M Package to Ex-Tesla, Aurora Exec as Product Chief, Filing Shows
articles.summary: General Motors offered a $40 million compensation package to recruit Sterling Anderson, formerly of Tesla and Aurora Innovation, as its Chief Product Officer. The package includes $16 million received in 2025 and an additional $24 million tied to performance targets and continued employment through 2027. This significant hiring and compensation package underscores GM's commitment to advancing its software and product development capabilities in the competitive EV market.

articles.title: As Lam Research Joins the Tesla Terafab, Should You Buy, Sell, or Hold LRCX Stock?
articles.summary: Lam Research (LRCX) stock has seen significant growth, up over 318% in the last 52 weeks, driven by the booming semiconductor industry and recent news of potential collaboration with Elon Musk's Terafab project. This partnership, aimed at advanced 2-nanometer chip production, could be a major growth accelerator for Lam Research, which is a leading supplier of wafer fabrication equipment. Analysts maintain a "Strong Buy" rating for LRCX, forecasting substantial earnings growth and continued value creation in the expanding WFE market.

articles.title: Asset Allocation Summit: Opportunities in Direxion’s Leveraged-Inverse Suite
articles.summary: During VettaFi's Asset Allocation Summit, Direxion's Edward Egilinsky discussed the growing opportunities in their leveraged-inverse ETF suite, especially given recent market volatility. He emphasized the critical importance of investor education due to the complex, derivatives-based nature of these products, which have been pioneered by Direxion since 2008. The summit highlighted both popular single-stock leveraged/inverse ETFs for companies like Tesla, Nvidia, and Micron, and also products for long-term investors like the Direxion NASDAQ-100 Equal Weighted Index ETF (QQQE).
